methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

data.narrative:
Genespire is a Series B biotech & health company founded by Luigi Naldini and Alessio Cantore, having raised $52M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, including Chai Discovery ($3.8B Series B), Eikon Therapeutics ($539M Series B), Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B Series C), and New Limit ($3.1B Series C).

Two valuation methods were applicable. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, using the $52M total raised as a proxy base, yielding $99M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation across the four sampled peers—$3.5B—with a range spanning $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied because Genespire's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $84M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity in the comp set and material uncertainty around Genespire's commercial traction relative to later-stage peers. Stage-progression suggests a conservative floor; peer-median weighting reflects the broader biotech market. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory, proprietary IP defensibility, and fundraising velocity within the current financing environment.
